Scenario.title: Operation Ghost Energy Sector Cyber Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex Operation Ghost is a sophisticated cyber attack targeting the energy sector, utilizing multiple tactics and techniques to achieve maximum impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the energy company's infrastructure and personnel by searching public sources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers create a compromised server or website to host malware and other tools.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Spear Phishing via Compromised Email Account
stage.description: The attackers trick employees into opening a malicious email attachment or link using spear phishing.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers use PowerShell to execute malicious scripts on compromised systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain access and evade detection.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in publicly facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers obfuscate malware code to evade detection by security software.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to guess passwords for accounts with high privilege levels.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network to identify potential targets and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers use valid credentials to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate stolen data over alternative protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt critical systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: Operation Ghost is a complex cyber attack targeting the energy sector, utilizing multiple tactics and techniques to achieve maximum impact. The attackers gather information, compromise infrastructure, gain initial access, execute malicious scripts, persist, escalate privileges, evade defense, access credentials, discover vulnerabilities, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate data, and finally cause significant disruption to critical systems.
